content:
FINAL RORK DEPENDENCY REMOVAL — IVX FULL OWNERSHIP, INDEPENDENT BUILD,
DEPLOYMENT, AI, AND PRODUCTION VERIFICATION

This is a production separation and ownership-transfer task.

Objective:

Remove every Rork dependency from IVX end-to-end so the application can be
developed, built, deployed, operated, monitored, and recovered entirely from
owner-controlled infrastructure.

Do not only rename files.
Do not only remove visible Rork branding.
Do not only change one Git remote.
Do not leave hidden runtime, build, routing, AI, deployment, EAS, Metro,
environment, package, or artifact dependencies.

The final system must operate without access to Rork accounts, Rork cloud,
Rork repositories, Rork runtime, Rork proxy, Rork Metro configuration,
Rork deployment routers, or Rork-managed secrets.

==================================================
PHASE 1 — FREEZE, BACKUP, AND CREATE ROLLBACK
==================================================

Before making changes:

1. Record the current production GitHub SHA.
2. Record the current Render deployed SHA.
3. Record the Android build SHA.
4. Record the web build SHA.
5. Record the current package-lock/bun lock checksum.
6. Export all production environment variable names and statuses.
7. Back up the production database.
8. Export current database schema and RLS policies.
9. Archive the current repository.
10. Archive current Android/iOS configuration.
11. Archive current deployment configuration.
12. Create a rollback branch and rollback tag.
13. Do not delete any historical code until the independent build passes.

Return:

CURRENT_REPOSITORY=
CURRENT_BRANCH=
CURRENT_HEAD_SHA=
CURRENT_RENDER_SHA=
CURRENT_ANDROID_SHA=
CURRENT_WEB_SHA=
ROLLBACK_BRANCH=
ROLLBACK_TAG=
DATABASE_BACKUP=
ARCHIVE_LOCATION=

==================================================
PHASE 2 — DISCOVER EVERY RORK DEPENDENCY
==================================================

Search every repository, branch, file, build artifact, environment, and
deployment configuration for case-insensitive references to:

- rork
- @rork
- rork-ai
- rork cloud
- rork proxy
- rork runtime
- rork sdk
- rork router
- rork metro
- rork build
- rork deploy
- rork project
- rork app
- rork.com
- EXPO_PUBLIC_RORK
- RORK_
- withRorkMetro
- rork.json
- .rork
- rork-generated
- rork-managed
- rork-testing
- Rork1
- Rork Git router
- Rork Fast
- Rork Max

Search:

- package.json
- lockfiles
- metro.config.js
- app.json
- app.config.*
- eas.json
- android/
- ios/
- scripts/
- backend/
- frontend/
- Expo config
- Babel config
- Gradle files
- Podfiles
- environment files
- Render settings
- GitHub workflows
- EAS settings
- Vercel settings
- DNS
- build artifacts
- generated bundles
- source maps
- README and scripts
- runtime logs
- API routes
- AI provider routing
- deployment hooks
- Git remotes

For every match return:

FILE
LINE
REFERENCE
CATEGORY
PRODUCTION_REACHABLE
REPLACEMENT_REQUIRED
ACTION
STATUS

Categories:

- package dependency
- build dependency
- runtime dependency
- API dependency
- AI dependency
- deployment dependency
- repository dependency
- environment dependency
- configuration dependency
- branding only
- historical documentation
- dead code

==================================================
PHASE 3 — REMOVE RORK PACKAGE AND SDK DEPENDENCIES
==================================================

Audit all direct and transitive dependencies.

Remove or replace:

- Rork SDK packages
- Rork runtime libraries
- Rork UI wrappers
- Rork router packages
- Rork Metro plugins
- Rork build helpers
- Rork AI proxy clients
- Rork deployment clients
- Rork analytics packages
- Rork authentication helpers
- Rork storage helpers
- Rork-generated native modules

For every removed package:

PACKAGE
VERSION
DIRECT_OR_TRANSITIVE
USED_BY_FILES
REPLACEMENT
SECURITY_IMPACT
BUILD_RESULT

After removal:

1. Regenerate lockfile.
2. Run dependency tree audit.
3. Confirm no Rork package remains.
4. Confirm no unavailable private registry dependency remains.
5. Confirm clean install works without Rork authentication.

Required proof:

npm/bun install from a clean environment must succeed with no Rork account.

==================================================
PHASE 4 — REPLACE RORK METRO AND EXPO CONFIGURATION
==================================================

Replace Rork-specific Metro and Expo configuration with owner-controlled,
standard Expo/React Native configuration.

Remove:

- withRorkMetro
- Rork Metro wrappers
- Rork resolver aliases
- Rork transformer plugins
- Rork bundle hooks
- Rork tunnel assumptions
- Rork project metadata
- Rork runtime conditionals
- Rork dev launcher logic

Verify:

- Metro starts with standard configuration.
- Android release builds without Metro.
- Production app contains bundled JavaScript.
- No Rork config is required at build time.
- No Rork config is required at runtime.
- No exp:// or Rork tunnel URL is used in production.

==================================================
PHASE 5 — TRANSFER GIT OWNERSHIP
==================================================

The only authoritative repository must be owner-controlled.

Required target:

OWNER_GITHUB_ACCOUNT=ibb142
OWNER_PRODUCTION_REPOSITORY=rork-global-real-estate-invest
TARGET_BRANCH=main

Audit all Git remotes:

- origin
- upstream
- deploy
- mirror
- Rork remotes
- temporary remotes

Actions:

1. Set origin to the owner repository.
2. Remove Rork remotes.
3. Remove Rork deploy router references.
4. Preserve full commit history.
5. Preserve tags and branches where valid.
6. Push all required production history.
7. Confirm owner has admin rights.
8. Confirm branch protection is owner-controlled.
9. Confirm deploy keys belong to owner infrastructure.
10. Confirm GitHub token belongs to owner account.
11. Confirm no GitHub App controlled by Rork is required.
12. Confirm no Rork webhook can mutate the repository.

Return:

FINAL_ORIGIN=
REMOVED_REMOTES=
DEFAULT_BRANCH=
HEAD_SHA=
OWNER_ACCESS_VERIFIED=
RORK_WEBHOOKS_REMOVED=
OWNER_DEPLOY_KEY_VERIFIED=

==================================================
PHASE 6 — TRANSFER RENDER DEPLOYMENT CONTROL
==================================================

Render must deploy directly from the owner GitHub repository.

Audit:

- connected repository
- connected branch
- service owner
- service environment
- deploy hook
- auto-deploy source
- build command
- start command
- secret source
- health endpoint
- custom domains

Actions:

1. Connect Render directly to the owner repository.
2. Connect to owner-controlled main branch.
3. Remove Rork Git router.
4. Remove Rork deploy hooks.
5. Remove Rork webhook dependencies.
6. Preserve environment variables.
7. Preserve service ID where possible.
8. Trigger deploy from owner GitHub push.
9. Confirm deployed SHA matches GitHub HEAD.
10. Confirm rollback works from owner-controlled commit.
11. Confirm service can redeploy when Rork is unavailable.

Required proof:

GITHUB_HEAD_SHA
=
RENDER_DEPLOYED_SHA
=
BACKEND_COMMIT_MARKER

==================================================
PHASE 7 — REMOVE RORK AI CLOUD AND PROXY DEPENDENCIES
==================================================

IVX AI must use owner-controlled provider accounts and backend routing.

Remove:

- Rork AI Cloud
- Rork proxy
- Rork model routing
- Rork API keys
- Rork fallback
- Rork provider selection
- Rork-specific request/response formats
- Rork billing dependency

Required AI architecture:

Android/Web
→ api.ivxholding.com
→ owner-controlled backend
→ owner-controlled AI provider
→ Supabase persistence
→ realtime response
→ client UI

Allowed providers:

- Vercel AI Gateway under owner account
- OpenAI under owner account
- Anthropic under owner account
- Google AI under owner account
- another owner-approved provider

Rules:

1. No client-side provider secret.
2. No Rork provider endpoint.
3. No Rork billing.
4. No Rork fallback.
5. No Rork proxy headers.
6. Provider key loaded from IVX encrypted vault or owner-controlled runtime.
7. Provider validation returns HTTP 200.
8. Real AI responses persist in Supabase.
9. 20-message E2E test passes.
10. Restart/retry/persistence tests pass.

==================================================
PHASE 8 — REMOVE RORK ENVIRONMENT VARIABLES
==================================================

Inventory every environment variable containing:

- RORK
- EXPO_PUBLIC_RORK
- RORK_PROJECT
- RORK_RUNTIME
- RORK_API
- RORK_PROXY
- RORK_DEPLOY
- RORK_AI
- RORK_TOKEN
- RORK_BUILD
- RORK_ROUTE

For each variable:

VARIABLE
USED_BY
CURRENT_VALUE_PRESENT
PRODUCTION_REQUIRED
REPLACEMENT
REMOVED
TEST_RESULT

Do not delete a variable until all code references are removed.

After migration:

1. Remove unused Rork variables from vault.
2. Remove unused Rork variables from Render.
3. Remove unused Rork variables from Expo/EAS.
4. Remove unused Rork variables from web builds.
5. Confirm no production startup warning depends on them.

==================================================
PHASE 9 — INDEPENDENT EAS / ANDROID BUILD
==================================================

Create owner-controlled EAS and Android build configuration.

Requirements:

1. Initialize EAS under the owner account.
2. Create owner-controlled project ID.
3. Update app.json/app.config.
4. Update eas.json.
5. Preserve Android package name.
6. Preserve versionCode/versionName.
7. Preserve signing identity if owner-controlled.
8. Transfer or regenerate signing credentials safely if necessary.
9. Build debug APK.
10. Build signed release APK.
11. Build signed AAB.
12. Install APK on clean Android device.
13. Launch without Expo Go.
14. Launch without Metro.
15. Launch while Rork is unavailable.
16. Confirm production API.
17. Confirm owner login.
18. Confirm AI.
19. Confirm database.
20. Confirm media uploads.

Return:

EAS_OWNER=
EAS_PROJECT_ID=
ANDROID_PACKAGE=
VERSION_NAME=
VERSION_CODE=
APK_PATH=
AAB_PATH=
APK_SHA256=
AAB_SHA256=
SIGNING_FINGERPRINT=
CLEAN_INSTALL_RESULT=

==================================================
PHASE 10 — INDEPENDENT IOS BUILD
==================================================

Prepare iOS under owner-controlled accounts.

Audit:

- bundle identifier
- Apple Team
- App Store Connect
- certificates
- provisioning profiles
- push credentials
- associated domains
- Sign in with Apple
- iOS entitlements

Remove any Rork-controlled Apple identity or provisioning dependency.

If App Store credentials are unavailable, complete all code and build
configuration and return only the exact owner-controlled account action still
required.

Do not leave Rork as certificate owner or release controller.

==================================================
PHASE 11 — REPLACE RORK-SPECIFIC SERVICES
==================================================

For every capability previously provided by Rork, create or confirm an IVX
replacement:

RORK CAPABILITY → IVX REPLACEMENT

- project generation → owner GitHub source
- AI proxy → IVX backend AI gateway
- deployment router → GitHub-to-Render direct deploy
- Metro wrapper → standard Metro config
- runtime flags → IVX runtime config
- app variables → IVX encrypted vault
- build pipeline → owner EAS/Gradle
- proof system → IVX Supabase proof ledger
- live task stream → IVX backend task stream
- patch approval → IVX owner approval API
- logs → IVX audit/log services
- storage → Supabase/S3 owner accounts
- analytics → owner-controlled analytics
- notifications → owner-controlled providers

For every replacement return:

OLD_RORK_COMPONENT
NEW_IVX_COMPONENT
FILES_CHANGED
DATA_MIGRATED
LIVE_TEST
STATUS

==================================================
PHASE 12 — AUDIT IVX SENIOR DEVELOPER INDEPENDENCE
==================================================

Prove IVX IA can operate without Rork.

Run one safe, real issue through:

1. User reports a bug.
2. IVX IA analyzes repository.
3. IVX IA identifies exact file.
4. IVX IA creates patch.
5. IVX IA displays diff.
6. Owner approves.
7. IVX IA applies patch.
8. IVX IA runs tests.
9. IVX IA commits to owner GitHub.
10. IVX IA pushes to main.
11. GitHub triggers Render.
12. Render deploys.
13. IVX IA verifies /health.
14. IVX IA verifies changed route.
15. IVX IA stores evidence in Supabase.
16. IVX IA returns commit SHA and deploy ID.
17. IVX IA demonstrates rollback readiness.

The test must not use:

- Rork interface actions
- Rork Git router
- Rork cloud
- Rork proxy
- Rork deploy hook
- Rork runtime
- build-marker-only changes

==================================================
PHASE 13 — SECURITY AND OWNERSHIP AUDIT
==================================================

Verify Rork no longer has:

- repository admin access
- deploy access
- Render access
- EAS access
- Apple signing access
- Google Play signing access
- Supabase owner access
- AWS access
- DNS access
- secret access
- production database mutation access
- webhook access
- GitHub App access
- deploy-key access
- service-account access

Do not expose secrets.

Return:

SYSTEM
OWNER_ACCOUNT
RORK_ACCESS_PRESENT
ACTION_TAKEN
FINAL_OWNER
STATUS

Rotate credentials only where access history proves Rork could still use them.

Do not rotate every credential blindly.

==================================================
PHASE 14 — CLEAN INSTALL AND BUILD AUDIT
==================================================

Use a clean machine/container with no Rork authentication.

Perform:

1. Clone owner repository.
2. Install dependencies.
3. Typecheck.
4. Lint.
5. Unit tests.
6. Integration tests.
7. Backend tests.
8. Android Gradle clean.
9. Assemble release APK.
10. Build AAB.
11. Build web.
12. Start backend.
13. Verify production configuration.
14. Confirm no Rork login prompt.
15. Confirm no private Rork package request.
16. Confirm no Rork environment dependency.

Return every command, exit code, duration, and logs.

==================================================
PHASE 15 — FULL APPLICATION REGRESSION
==================================================

Verify all critical modules after Rork removal:

- Owner Login
- Admin HQ
- Access Control
- Members
- Investors
- Buyers
- JV Deals
- Influencers
- Realtors
- Brokers
- Tokenized Investors
- IVX Staff
- Properties
- Deals
- CRM
- Wallet
- Transactions
- Revenue
- Fees
- Chat
- IVX Owner AI
- Developer Workspace
- Variables
- Security Box
- Media
- Notifications
- Landing Control
- Registration
- Analytics
- Email
- Backup
- Data Recovery
- Deploy Control

For every module return:

MODULE
ANDROID_RESULT
WEB_RESULT
API_RESULT
DATABASE_RESULT
OWNER_AUTH_RESULT
RORK_DEPENDENCY_FOUND
FINAL_STATUS

==================================================
PHASE 16 — DEPLOYMENT AND SHA CERTIFICATION
==================================================

Required equality:

OWNER_GITHUB_HEAD_SHA
=
RENDER_DEPLOYED_SHA
=
BACKEND_COMMIT_MARKER
=
ANDROID_BUILD_SHA
=
WEB_BUILD_SHA

Return:

REPOSITORY
BRANCH
HEAD_SHA
RENDER_SERVICE
RENDER_DEPLOY_ID
RENDER_SHA
HEALTH_URL
HEALTH_STATUS
ANDROID_SHA
WEB_SHA
BUILD_TIMESTAMP

==================================================
PHASE 17 — REMOVE RORK ACCESS AND FINAL CLEANUP
==================================================

Only after clean builds and live verification:

1. Remove Rork packages.
2. Remove Rork config.
3. Remove Rork environment variables.
4. Remove Rork remotes.
5. Remove Rork webhooks.
6. Remove Rork deploy hooks.
7. Remove Rork service accounts.
8. Remove Rork GitHub Apps.
9. Remove Rork team access.
10. Remove Rork runtime code.
11. Remove Rork proxy code.
12. Remove Rork branding where desired.
13. Preserve historical references only in archived documentation.
14. Confirm rollback remains available in the owner repository.

==================================================
FINAL EVIDENCE PACKAGE
==================================================

DEPENDENCY AUDIT

- total Rork references found
- runtime references removed
- build references removed
- deployment references removed
- AI references removed
- repository references removed
- environment references removed
- historical documentation references retained

OWNERSHIP

- GitHub owner
- Render owner
- Supabase owner
- AWS owner
- EAS owner
- Apple owner/status
- Google Play owner/status
- DNS owner

BUILD

- clean install result
- Android release result
- APK
- AAB
- web build result
- iOS build/configuration result

DEPLOYMENT

- final commit SHA
- Render deploy ID
- deployed SHA
- health HTTP status
- direct GitHub auto-deploy test
- rollback test

AI

- provider
- owner account
- HTTP 200 validation
- 20-message E2E result
- persistence result
- no Rork fallback

IVX IA

- patch execution result
- GitHub push result
- Render deploy result
- production verification
- proof-ledger result
- no Rork dependency

==================================================
FINAL VERDICT
==================================================

Return exactly one:

RORK DEPENDENCY REMOVAL COMPLETE — IVX OWNER CONTROL VERIFIED

or

RORK DEPENDENCY REMOVAL INCOMPLETE

Do not state COMPLETE unless:

- no production Rork package remains;
- no Rork runtime remains;
- no Rork AI proxy remains;
- no Rork deploy router remains;
- no Rork Git remote remains;
- no Rork environment dependency remains;
- clean install works without Rork credentials;
- Android release builds independently;
- web builds independently;
- GitHub deploys directly to Render;
- IVX AI uses owner-controlled provider infrastructure;
- IVX IA can patch, commit, deploy, and verify independently;
- owner controls GitHub, Render, database, build, signing, and secrets;
- critical modules pass regression;
- final evidence is complete.

If incomplete, return only:

RORK_DEPENDENCIES_REMAINING:
FILES:
PACKAGES:
ENVIRONMENT_VARIABLES:
GIT_REMOTES:
WEBHOOKS:
DEPLOYMENT_DEPENDENCIES:
AI_DEPENDENCIES:
BUILD_DEPENDENCIES:
OWNER_ACTION_REQUIRED:
EXACT_NEXT_ACTION:
